_meta.attested_errors_caught: 1. Threadline description was wrong — corrected from 'Clinical AI Persona Pipeline' to accurate adaptive fashion market intelligence pipeline description. 2. Programming languages were miscategorized inside ai_technical — moved to a separate 'languages' skill category. 3. Threadline start date was wrong — corrected to May 2026, project is ongoing.

education.degree: Master of Science in Information Systems
coursework: User Experience in the Era of AI

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics & Communication

experience.title: Technical Product Manager Intern
bullets: Owned 0-to-1 product discovery for a cervical cancer early-detection platform; defined product strategy and end-to-end roadmap, built user personas, synthesized competitive landscape (Function Health, Color Health), and translated clinical requirements into ML model specs for cancer risk stratification.

Spearheaded growth product management initiatives by establishing SEO infrastructure from zero; configured GA4, GSC, and SEMrush for the first time, implemented on-page optimization and keyword strategy, and executed full website migration from WordPress to Framer via Git/SSH.

Drove moderated UX research and usability sessions; translated findings into a full rebuild of the company's landing website on Framer, redesigning onboarding flows and reducing friction; managed product backlog and led Agile sprints to deliver milestones on time.

Developed brand messaging, homepage and employer-facing B2B copy, and content calendar; created and launched company's Instagram and Facebook accounts from scratch to establish digital presence ahead of product launch.

experience.title: Development Engineer
bullets: Reduced UI drop-off rates by 25% and increased session duration by 2+ minutes on the Straight2Bank B2B trade finance platform by analyzing user interaction patterns and applying voice of customer insights to prioritize feature improvements across 3+ global regions with localized compliance requirements.

Defined user stories, acceptance criteria, and feature specifications for merchant-facing payment workflows; built and maintained SQL dashboards and data pipelines enabling data-driven release planning for payment and cash management features.
Directed Sprint Planning and Agile ceremonies across cross-functional teams using Jira; increased release velocity by 30% over two quarters.

experience.title: Data Engineer Intern
bullets: Mapped and documented end-to-end hardware Product Lifecycle Management for a facial recognition integration system deployed across Indian banking infrastructure; tracked iteration cycles, component changes, and integration testing across embedded sensor systems.
Streamlined product analytics workflows across 3 product iterations; reduced time-to-insight for product leads by consolidating cross-team documentation and change logs.
Built internal flowcharts and schema documentation for real-time IoT telemetry and data ingestion pipelines supporting edge analytics on embedded sensor hardware.

projects.name: TripSync
bullets: Conducted 3-phase UX research study with 10 user interviews per phase; built 11-category, 69-code qualitative framework synthesizing 220 user needs into a 327-row prioritized product backlog with user stories and acceptance criteria.

Designed low-fidelity and high-fidelity Figma prototypes and built working product on Lovable.dev; ran 5 moderated usability sessions achieving 100% task success rate and avg score of 9.62 out of 10 (above 8.5 SUS threshold).

Identified top friction points including AI itinerary post-generation transition and duplicate activity indicator confusion; translated findings into actionable UX requirements and PRD v1.0 for a collaborative group trip planning app.

Conducted comparative evaluation of low-fidelity prototype feedback across 10 real user interviews vs AI-generated analysis; found AI identified more feature gaps while real users surfaced interaction complexity; synthesized findings into a change log to inform high-fidelity iteration.

projects.name: Threadline — Adaptive Fashion Market Intelligence Pipeline
bullets: Building an adaptive fashion market intelligence pipeline that analyzes behavioral signals from product reviews, return data, and community forums to identify underserved consumer segments — specifically post-mastectomy survivors and elderly women with limited hand mobility.

Surfaces viable product opportunities in the adaptive garment market by clustering real purchase and sentiment data into actionable insights for product teams; implemented via n8n workflow orchestration, Claude API, and state-based routing logic.
Managing full product lifecycle from requirements definition through GitHub Pages deployment using Python, JavaScript, Figma, and Git.

projects.name: Quality First Initiative
bullets: Served as Project Lead and Primary Research Analyst; conducted primary interview with VP of Product Delivery to ground a change management plan for a healthcare fintech startup scaling from 20 to 70 employees.

Applied ADKAR and McKinsey 7S frameworks across 8 stakeholder groups; identified 3 critical process gaps — undefined non-functional testing standards, misaligned sprint requirements, and absent working agreements.

Delivered 4-part recommendation with requirement templates, QA checklists, formalized working agreements, and a centralized process repository; supported by a 9-KPI success metrics framework tracking rework rate, defect detection timing, and sprint predictability.

projects.name: TESLA Algorithmic Trading Bot
bullets: Built an ensemble ML trading system (LASSO + XGBoost + HAC-OLS) on 99 engineered features from TESLA OHLCV data, 22 competitor equities, Fama-French 5-factor model, RSI, Bollinger Bands, MACD, and VIX sentiment; achieved 91% prediction accuracy and 0.996 prediction-actual correlation.

Implemented walk-forward validation (252-day rolling window), RobustScaler preprocessing, and systematic lag transformations to prevent data leakage; reduced 99 features to 28 significant predictors (p≤0.05).

Outperformed Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-B) by 15% over 1,187 trading days (37.41% vs 22.41% total return); achieved Sharpe ratio of 2.17 vs benchmark 1.52 and 62% drawdown reduction via adaptive position sizing and dynamic stop-loss.

projects.name: University Event Management System
bullets: Managed end-to-end development of a university event platform coordinating a 5-member Agile team; delivered a scalable system serving the entire university student body across multiple events per semester, supporting participant tracking, venue booking, ticketing, and sponsorship management.
Led project planning, timeline definition, and task assignments using Agile practices; ensured on-time delivery of all key modules aligned with stakeholder needs.
Designed and implemented database schema supporting multi-event tracking, role-based access, and real-time participant updates across concurrent events.

leadership.note: Managed 400-member organizing team; coordinated 2,000+ participants and secured $40,000 in sponsorships for the university's largest cultural event.
Managed technical and cultural events overseeing logistics and participant engagement across 8,000+ participants.
Supported speaker coordination and event logistics for 400+ attendee summit focused on women in AI.

publications.title: Smart Headgear for Rider Protection from Intoxication and Emergency System
Plant Disease Detection using Deep Learning Concepts
